mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate keeps the equity discount rate anchored, so SPX tends to rise over the next month when policy is not tightening. This signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating faster than the policy rate.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with unchanged or falling FEDFUNDS do not produce higher 20d SPX returns than periods with rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the effect is negative in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the recent trend, real discount rates and valuation pressure rise faster than nominal sales growth, leading to negative SPX returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration triggers are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average post-trigger return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and lower future consumption, which compresses earnings expectations and pushes SPX lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns after a 0.3pp/3m unemployment rise are not materially below baseline, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55% across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion accompanied by a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-income growth, supporting household demand and positive SPX performance over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not outperform either series alone on 60d forward returns, or if the combined signal loses predictive sign in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending, which supports revenue growth and margin stability for large-cap equities, producing positive SPX returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change is positive and above its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth triggers do not produce above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the sign becomes negative when RSAFS growth is above its own median.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and earnings leverage, which should weigh on cyclicals and pull SPX lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling INDPRO does not precede below-average 60d SPX returns, or if the negative effect disappears in consecutive 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both rise together, housing activity and retail demand confirm a broad demand upturn that lifts SPX over the next quarter; if only one rises, the signal is weaker and should not fire.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in HOUST and RSAFS does not outperform each series individually, or if the combined signal fails to generate positive average 60d SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a combination of above-trend CPIAUCSL and a rising FEDFUNDS rate raises real-rate pressure and multiple compression, producing negative SPX returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is rising and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is above 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-plus-tightening trigger does not produce negative 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal is not stronger than either CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.
